title: Manager: URL-based tag filter state + filter-aware initial story selection
body:
Closes #34252

<!-- If your PR is related to an issue, provide the number(s) above; if it resolves multiple issues, be sure to break them up (e.g. "closes #1000, closes #1001"). -->

<!--

Thank you for contributing to Storybook! Please submit all PRs to the `next` branch unless they are specific to the current release. Storybook maintainers cherry-pick bug and documentation fixes into the `main` branch as part of the release process, so you shouldn't need to worry about this. For additional guidance: https://storybook.js.org/docs/contribute

-->

## What I did

Replaces localStorage persistence for tag filters with URL-based state. All filter state is now encoded in a single `tags` URL parameter (e.g. `?tags=a11y;$changed;!experimental`). Also fixes the bug where initial story selection ignored active tag filters when navigating to Storybook without a story path.


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e39e00d2-39c7-4147-9e95-b278de17f6c1


## Checklist for Contributors

### Testing

<!-- Please check (put an "x" inside the "[ ]") the applicable items below to communicate how to test your changes -->

#### The changes in this PR are covered in the following automated tests:

- [ ] stories
- [x] unit tests
- [ ] integration tests
- [ ] end-to-end tests

#### Manual testing

> [!CAUTION]
> This section is mandatory for all contributions. If you believe no manual test is necessary, please state so explicitly. Thanks!

1. Run a sandbox: `yarn task --task sandbox --start-from auto --template react-vite/default-ts`
2. Add a tag filter via the sidebar filter UI (e.g. include `play`)
3. Verify the URL updates to `?tags=$play` (or the appropriate tag encoding)
4. Refresh the page — verify the same filter is restored from the URL
5. Navigate to `http://localhost:6006/?tags=play` directly — verify only stories tagged `play` are shown and the first story/docs is auto-selected
6. Clear all filters — verify the `tags` param is removed from the URL
7. Click between sidebar entries repeatedly — verify `tags` param is preserved in the URL throughout

### Documentation

<!-- Please check (put an "x" inside the "[ ]") the applicable items below to indicate which documentation has been updated. -->

- [ ] Add or update documentation reflecting your changes
- [ ] If you are deprecating/removing a feature, make sure to update
      [MIGRATION.MD](https://github.com/storybookjs/storybook/blob/next/MIGRATION.md)

## Checklist for Maintainers

- [ ] When this PR is ready for testing, make sure to add `ci:normal`, `ci:merged` or `ci:daily` GH label to it to run a specific set of sandboxes. The particular set of sandboxes can be found in `code/lib/cli-storybook/src/sandbox-templates.ts`
- [ ] Make sure this PR contains **one** of the labels below:
   <details>
     <summary>Available labels</summary>
  - `bug`: Internal changes that fixes incorrect behavior.
  - `maintenance`: User-facing maintenance tasks.
  - `dependencies`: Upgrading (sometimes downgrading) dependencies.
  - `build`: Internal-facing build tooling & test updates. Will not show up in release changelog.
  - `cleanup`: Minor cleanup style change. Will not show up in release changelog.
  - `documentation`: Documentation **only** changes. Will not show up in release changelog.
  - `feature request`: Introducing a new feature.
  - `BREAKING CHANGE`: Changes that break compatibility in some way with current major version.
  - `other`: Changes that don't fit in the above categories.

   </details>

### 🦋 Canary release

<!-- CANARY_RELEASE_SECTION -->

This PR does not have a canary release associated. You can request a canary release of this pull request by mentioning the `@storybookjs/core` team here.

_core team members can create a canary release [here](https://github.com/storybookjs/storybook/actions/workflows/publish.yml) or locally with `gh workflow run --repo storybookjs/storybook publish.yml --field pr=<PR_NUMBER>`_

<!-- CANARY_RELEASE_SECTION -->

<!-- BENCHMARK_SECTION -->
<!-- BENCHMARK_SECTION -->


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Tag filters now persist in the URL (bookmarkable/shareable) and are initialized from the URL on load
  * Query parameters are preserved during story navigation so filters/selections remain intact
  * Story selection respects active tag filters and will navigate to the first matching story/docs entry when needed
  * URL-backed persistence supports explicit adding/removing of query params (null clears a param)
  * Safer URL/query handling on load and navigation (handles missing location/search)

* **Tests**
  * Added tests for tag filter parsing/serialization and URL query-parameter behavior, including navigation expectations
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
author: valentinpalkovic

comments.author: coderabbitai
comments.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**Use the matched entry type for the filtered fallback.**

`firstFiltered` can be a docs entry, but this redirect always reuses the preview-emitted `viewMode`. With docs-only results such as `?tags=$docs`, that can produce `/story/<docsId>` instead of `/docs/<docsId>`. Either restrict the fallback to stories or derive the route from the matched entry itself.

<details>
<summary>Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
-              const firstFiltered = filteredIndex
-                ? Object.keys(filteredIndex).find((id) => {
-                    const entry = filteredIndex[id];
-                    return entry.type === 'story' || entry.type === 'docs';
-                  })
-                : undefined;
+              const firstFiltered = filteredIndex
+                ? Object.values(filteredIndex).find(
+                    (entry) => entry.type === 'story' || entry.type === 'docs'
+                  )
+                : undefined;
 
               if (firstFiltered) {
-                navigateWithQueryParams(`/${viewMode}/${firstFiltered}`);
+                navigateWithQueryParams(`/${firstFiltered.type}/${firstFiltered.id}`);
               }
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@code/core/src/manager-api/modules/stories.ts` around lines 967 - 975, The
fallback redirect uses viewMode blindly which can misroute a docs entry; change
the fallback to derive the route from filteredIndex[firstFiltered]. Retrieve
const entry = filteredIndex[firstFiltered]; determine routeMode = entry &&
entry.type === 'docs' ? 'docs' : 'story' (or only allow 'story' if you prefer
restricting), then call
navigateWithQueryParams(`/${routeMode}/${firstFiltered}`) instead of using
viewMode; ensure you guard for entry existence before navigating.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:grasshopper -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

comments.author: coderabbitai
comments.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**Legacy localStorage can still override these URL-derived filters.**

These values are only correct if nothing later overwrites them. `Store.getInitialState()` in `code/core/src/manager-api/store.ts` still merges `store.local` and `store.session` over the module state on startup, so previously persisted `includedTagFilters` / `excludedTagFilters` will win over `initialIncluded` / `initialExcluded` for existing users. That leaves the active filters out of sync with the `tags` URL until the user changes them again. This migration needs a one-time scrub or an explicit ignore for the legacy persisted keys.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@code/core/src/manager-api/modules/stories.ts` around lines 1179 - 1183, The
URL-parsed tag filters (computed via queryFromLocation and parseTagsParam into
initialIncluded/initialExcluded when hasTagsParam is true) are being overridden
by legacy persisted keys merged by Store.getInitialState (store.local /
store.session) so the active filters stay out-of-sync; modify startup behavior
so when hasTagsParam is true you ignore or scrub legacy persisted
includedTagFilters/excludedTagFilters instead of letting Store.getInitialState
merge them: either (A) update Store.getInitialState to skip merging those two
keys from store.local/session when a tags param is present, or (B) add a
one-time migration in the stories module startup to delete/clear
store.local.storeKeys for includedTagFilters/excludedTagFilters before state
merge, or (C) ensure the module’s initial state explicitly sets
includedTagFilters/excludedTagFilters to parsedTags.included/parsedTags.excluded
after Store.getInitialState runs when hasTagsParam is true so URL-derived values
win.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:grasshopper -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

✅ Addressed in commit c9da280

comments.author: coderabbitai
comments.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**Preserve an explicit empty tag selection.**

`parseTagsParam('')` already means “no filters”, but returning `undefined` here turns that into “no `tags` param”. `code/core/src/manager-api/modules/stories.ts` treats an absent `tags` param as “use preset defaults”, so clearing the last active filter cannot round-trip when any preset has `defaultFilterSelection`. Please keep a distinct empty serialization and make sure the URL-writing path preserves empty strings instead of dropping them.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@code/core/src/manager-api/modules/tags.ts` around lines 39 - 42, The
serialization currently returns undefined when both included and excluded are
empty which collapses an explicit “no tags” selection into an absent tags param;
change serializeTagsParam (in code/core/src/manager-api/modules/tags.ts) to
return an explicit empty string ('') when included.length===0 &&
excluded.length===0 so the URL-writing path can preserve the empty value instead
of dropping the param, and ensure this aligns with parseTagsParam so
parseTagsParam('') continues to mean “no filters” while undefined remains “no
tags param / use presets” (verify any callers in stories.ts that write the tags
param do not treat '' as missing).
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:grasshopper -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

✅ Addressed in commit c9da280

comments.author: coderabbitai
coderabbitai
coderabbitai
comments.body:
_🛠️ Refactor suggestion_ | _🟠 Major_

<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
# Verify direct mock mutation and beforeEach presence in this test file
rg -n "store2\\.(session|local)\\.get\\s*\\.?\\s*mockReturnValueOnce|store2\\.(session|local)\\.get\\.mockReturnValueOnce" code/core/src/manager-api/tests/store.test.js
rg -n "\\bbeforeEach\\s*\\(" code/core/src/manager-api/tests/store.test.js
```

Repository: storybookjs/storybook

Length of output: 114

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
cat -n code/core/src/manager-api/tests/store.test.js | head -100
```

Repository: storybookjs/storybook

Length of output: 4219

---



</details>

**Move mock setup to use `vi.mock(..., { spy: true })` at top level and configure mock behaviors in `beforeEach` using `vi.mocked()` accessors.**

Lines 24-30 configure mock behaviors inline within the test body and use direct function access instead of `vi.mocked()` handles. Additionally, the `vi.mock('store2', ...)` call at line 8 is missing the required `spy: true` option. Please refactor:

1. Add `spy: true` to the `vi.mock()` call on line 8
2. Introduce a `beforeEach` block to reset mocks
3. Use `vi.mocked()` to wrap `store2.session.get` and `store2.local.get` references

<details>
<summary>Suggested refactor</summary>

```diff
-vi.mock('store2', () => ({
+vi.mock('store2', { spy: true }, () => ({
   default: {
     local: {
       set: vi.fn(),
       get: vi.fn(),
     },
     session: {
       set: vi.fn(),
       get: vi.fn(),
     },
     _: { fn: () => {} },
   },
 }));

 describe('store', () => {
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    vi.mocked(store2.session.get).mockReset();
+    vi.mocked(store2.local.get).mockReset();
+  });
+
   it('sensibly combines local+session storage for initial state', () => {
     // Each storage is read twice: once for the migration check, once for the actual merge.
-    store2.session.get
+    vi.mocked(store2.session.get)
       .mockReturnValueOnce({ foo: 'bar', combined: { a: 'b' } })
       .mockReturnValueOnce({ foo: 'bar', combined: { a: 'b' } });
-    store2.local.get
+    vi.mocked(store2.local.get)
       .mockReturnValueOnce({ foo: 'baz', another: 'value', combined: { c: 'd' } })
       .mockReturnValueOnce({ foo: 'baz', another: 'value', combined: { c: 'd' } });
```
</details>

This aligns with the coding guidelines: use `vi.mock()` with `spy: true`, implement mock behaviors in `beforeEach` blocks, and use `vi.mocked()` to type-safely access mocked functions.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@code/core/src/manager-api/tests/store.test.js` around lines 24 - 30, Update
the test to use vi.mock(..., { spy: true }) for the store2 mock, and move the
inline mockReturnValueOnce setup into a beforeEach where you reset mocks (e.g.,
vi.resetAllMocks or vi.clearAllMocks) and then configure behaviors via
vi.mocked(...) wrappers; specifically replace direct calls to
store2.session.get.mockReturnValueOnce and store2.local.get.mockReturnValueOnce
with vi.mocked(store2.session.get).mockReturnValueOnce(...) and
vi.mocked(store2.local.get).mockReturnValueOnce(...), doing both the
migration-check and merge returns inside beforeEach so tests use the typed
vi.mocked accessors and mocks are reset between tests.
```

</details>
